hard.next_step: Jun 29, 2026 Proposed Post a 7-yrs contract for S2C and Procurement as an option (guaranteed for 24 months) Next step: Meeting with Pölki and CPO (Mr Flandorfer) on July 1st
hard.ais_why: Product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake and AppXtend across S2P + Intake with extensive S2P modules; opp name explicitly calls out Intake; EY-advised RFP process; multi-signal AI Hungry profile.

forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (re-dated: realistic close is late Q3 2026 at the earliest)
forecast_read.reason: The deal is genuinely live and engaged, so this is a date problem, not a deal problem: the CPO said on 1 Jul 2026 that the decision must first win an internal budget fight via a new business case on Austrian Post's digital roadmap, and that checkpoint has no date. A 23 Jul close cannot absorb business case build, controlling validation, roadmap checkpoint, IT green light and paper.
forecast_read.math: Today 2026-07-09, close_date 2026-07-23 = 14 days to close. In Shortlisted since 2026-04-30 (70 days). Close already pushed once, 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-23 on 22 Jun 2026, the same day the forecast was downgraded from Best Case to Upside Key Deal. Zycus told the buyer on 7 Jul that implementation must start mid-August to hit an end-of-year go-live; the buyer has not yet dated the roadmap checkpoint that gates the decision, so signature by 23 Jul is not achievable on current pace.

metrics.narrative: The buyer has named a target but not proven it: on the 7 Jul 2026 call Engelbert Pölki said 'we are expecting, for example, two percent cost saving over the spend, but we should prove it', and Zycus (John Woodcock) committed an internal team to co-build that business case with Post's numbers. The CPO Mathias Flandorfer made the stakes explicit on the 1 Jul onsite: 'the business cases are fighting against each other' on the digital roadmap and 'there is no real sense of urgency to change everything at the moment' because Post is already highly digitalized. Until the 2% savings case is quantified and validated with Post's controlling (now running in parallel per Pölki, 7 Jul), metrics remain a stated expectation, not a confirmed case.

economic_buyer.narrative: Power is mapped and now directly engaged, but not yet won. CPO Mathias Flandorfer (Executive Sponsor contact role) attended the 1 Jul 2026 Vienna onsite in person, probed the enterprise licence model, M&A scaling and hyperinflation exposure, and set the condition: he 'only wants to see a new business case from our side with the best prices we get from the market' before the project can advance (Pölki relaying his boss, 7 Jul). Salesforce MEDDPICC also names CFO Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner as the economic buyer with a cost-structure concern from the 1 Jul onsite that remains open. Flandorfer declined a CEO/CIO executive connect 'at the moment' (7 Jul), so EB engagement is real but conditional, not sponsoring.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and repeatedly buyer-voiced: price transparency and price security over time (Pölki needs 'a side-2-side comparison of the offers from Coupa and Zycus', Next Step 21 May 2026), functional comparability ('we can compare prices for functionality with prices for functionality', Flandorfer 1 Jul), flexibility on rollout timing because SAP resources are locked on the Turkey rollout (Flandorfer 1 Jul: 'it's not only the price, it's also the flexibility'), one integrated suite for the whole group 'from Austria to Azerbaijan', and validated use cases from the May-June POC and testing rounds. Zycus's transaction-cap on Merlin Intake is now being tested directly against the price-security criterion (Pölki, 7 Jul).

decision_process.narrative: The process is legible but the final gate is undated: shortlist confirmed (Zycus told 'you're already in the shortlist' on 1 Jul, and Flandorfer said Post is seeking 'a flexible model with everybody who is now in the shortlist'), then business case with controlling, then a ranking checkpoint on Post's internal digital roadmap ('we need to rank us there high, and then we get the resources', Flandorfer 1 Jul), then IT green light and CPO/CIO approval. Pölki committed on 7 Jul to find out when the next roadmap schedule is planned and report back. The gap is that no date exists for that checkpoint, which makes the whole downstream timeline unplannable.

paper_process.narrative: Paper mechanics are ahead of most Shortlisted deals: Zycus sent the Rahmenvertrag (MSA) and a standard Order Form with SLAs to Pölki on 18 May 2026, and the commercial structure on the table is a 7-year S2C contract with the procurement (P2P) module optional and prices guaranteed for 24 months after signature (Next Step 29 Jun; onsite 1 Jul). A call-off style flexible structure was discussed on the 25 Jun pricing call. No redlines or legal-to-legal activity has started, and none can start before the business case clears the roadmap checkpoint.
sources: SF Task 18 May 2026 'Zycus MSA und SLAs' (email to Pölki)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well established and consistent since the first call: the incumbent Allocation Network S2C tool is felt to be inadequate, 'insbesondere wegen des schlechten Supports nach der Übernahme durch QAT' (28 Apr 2025 intro call), and the Wallmedien WPS P2P system surfaced on the 1 Jul onsite as running an older version with uncertain API depth ('meine haben ganz schön gestaunt, als wir eine Spec von 2013 bekommen'). The group wants one suite across all countries. The complication is a second, internal pain that blocks the fix: SAP team resources are consumed by the Turkey S/4HANA rollout, so IT capacity for this project is scarce (Flandorfer, 1 Jul).
sources: Avoma 28 Apr 2025 intro call (prior record, verbatim)

champion.narrative: Engelbert Pölki (Manager Strategic Projects IT & Supply Chain, primary Decision Maker contact role) is an active, working champion: he negotiates pricing line by line, engaged controlling to co-validate the business case, and told Zycus on 7 Jul he had spoken to his boss within the last half hour about the project. Karin Eppich co-drives the technical track and voiced preference for Zycus on 16 Mar 2026. The limit is converted power: Pölki admitted on the 1 Jul onsite 'wir werden auch ein bissel hingehalten' (we are being held back a bit) by his own leadership, and his boss is withholding CIO engagement until the business case exists. Advocacy is real; sign-off authority is not yet moving.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the live rival at near price parity (475K vs Zycus's 448.68K, Next Step 21 Apr 2026) and had its own interface discussions with Post ('das ähnliche Gespräch auch schon mit den Kollegen von Coupa', onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul). The 11 Jun 2026 testing feedback put Zycus ahead on Source-to-Contract and AI but rivals slightly ahead on P2P usability. Tonkean surfaced 24 Jun 2026 as a low-cost bolt-on intake/orchestration option that exploits Post's SAP resource constraint. The quieter but real alternative is deferral: Flandorfer said the incumbent S2C contract can be extended another year, so do-nothing until 2027 is a funded fallback.
sources: Avoma 24 Jun 2026 AI-based Integration Alignment call (prior record)

critical_signals.text: CPO Mathias Flandorfer set a new gate on 1 Jul: the project only advances if a fresh business case with best market prices wins its slot on Austrian Post's internal digital roadmap, and that checkpoint has no date yet.

Pölki reviewed Zycus's revised pricing live on 7 Jul: Zycus accepted Post's proposed volumes on the core S2C modules, but Merlin Intake moved to a transaction-based model (18,000 transactions in 2027, unlimited users) that Post must now validate against its own usage.

The 1 Jul Vienna onsite put the CPO in the room for the first time; he engaged deeply on the enterprise licence, group-wide scaling and flexibility, but tied everything to IT capacity locked on the Turkey SAP rollout.
Coupa sits at near price parity (475K vs 449K) and has held its own interface talks with Post, while Tonkean is being floated as a cheap bolt-on that would let Post defer the full-suite decision.
Melissa Koller, Head of Purchasing Procure2Pay whose team will own the solution, joined the 1 Jul onsite and said she is 'already looking forward to next steps'.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely live, buyer-engaged shortlist fight: Zycus is price-competitive against Coupa, validated ahead on S2C and AI in testing, and now negotiating commercial structure directly with the CPO. What holds the position down is that the buyer has not selected anyone, the decision is gated on an undated internal budget checkpoint, the CIO is not engaged, and the 23 Jul close date is not achievable.

win_position.text: Zycus is confirmed in the final shortlist and is the vendor Post is actively negotiating structure with: a 7-year S2C contract with optional P2P and a 24-month price guarantee was tabled on 1 Jul 2026, and Pölki walked the revised pricing live with Zycus on 7 Jul.
Buyer-voiced preference signals are real: Karin Eppich expressed preference for Zycus on 16 Mar 2026, and the 11 Jun 2026 testing feedback put Zycus ahead on Source-to-Contract and AI capability.
Power is now in the room: CPO Mathias Flandorfer attended the 1 Jul Vienna onsite in person and engaged on licence model, scaling and flexibility rather than delegating.
No selection has been made: Flandorfer said on 1 Jul that Post is seeking a flexible model 'with everybody who is now in the shortlist', with Coupa at near price parity (475K vs 449K, 21 Apr 2026).

The decision is hostage to an internal budget fight: the business case must win an undated slot on Post's digital roadmap while SAP resources are locked on the Turkey rollout, and Flandorfer declined a CIO executive connect on 7 Jul.

The quantified business case Post's leadership demands does not exist yet: Pölki named a 2% savings-over-spend expectation on 7 Jul but said 'we should prove it', and Zycus only committed to co-build it that day.

deal_momentum.text: Two deep buyer sessions inside 8 days: the 1 Jul Vienna onsite (Teil 1 + 2) with the CPO, Pölki, Koller and Eppich, and a 7 Jul live pricing review where Pölki took the transaction model away for internal calculation.

The buyer is doing work between meetings: Pölki engaged controlling on the business case, spoke to his boss about the project on 7 Jul, and Eppich sent updated WPS documentation to Zycus around 6 Jul.
Cadence is dense and two-way: pricing and integration sessions on 9, 11, 16, 24 and 25 Jun plus buyer email replies on the spend-file audit (8-9 Jun).

The CRM tape moved against the deal on 22-25 Jun: forecast downgraded from Best Case to Upside Key Deal, close pushed from 30 Jun to 23 Jul, and amount cut from 650K to 448.68K as P2P was carved out as optional.
Forward motion now depends on a buyer-side gate with no date: Pölki must find out when the digital-roadmap schedule is planned and has not yet given a checkpoint date.

customer_commitment.text: Post committed real effort in the window: the CPO gave Zycus onsite time on 1 Jul, Pölki agreed on 7 Jul to calculate the 18,000-transaction cap internally and come back with figures, and he accepted a follow-up catch-up within the same week.

Pölki is spending internal capital: he engaged controlling to co-validate the business case and is pressing his own IT for the S2C interface investigation ('da machen wir jetzt auch Druck drauf', 1 Jul).

Leadership commitment is explicitly withheld: Pölki's boss 'only wants to see a new business case' and 'he don't want to get his CIO at the moment' (7 Jul), so buyer commitment stops below the approval line.

deal_risk.text: Budget-fight risk: with Post already highly digitalized there is 'no real sense of urgency' (Flandorfer, 1 Jul), business cases compete on the digital roadmap, and the incumbent S2C contract can be extended another year, making deferral to 2027 a funded fallback.

IT capacity risk: SAP team resources are consumed by the Turkey S/4HANA rollout and the following CE-country migrations, so even a won selection cannot mobilize without an IT green light that has not been given.
Close-date risk: 14 days to a 23 Jul close with no selection, no dated checkpoint, and Zycus itself telling the buyer implementation must start mid-August for an end-of-year go-live.

Pricing-model risk: the new transaction-based Merlin Intake cap collides with Pölki's core criterion of price security ('we have to close our business because there's no money' if the cap is exhausted mid-year, 7 Jul) until the definition, sizing and carryover questions are answered.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced: Karin Eppich expressed her preference for Zycus (16 Mar 2026, Next Step history); the 11 Jun 2026 testing feedback put Zycus ahead on Source-to-Contract and AI; Post gave Zycus a CPO-attended onsite on 1 Jul and a live pricing review on 7 Jul. Tempered because Flandorfer said on 1 Jul that Post is comparing 'everybody who is now in the shortlist' and Coupa sits at near price parity.

business_case.evidence: Flandorfer requires a new business case before the project can advance (relayed by Pölki, 7 Jul 2026); Pölki named a 2% savings-over-spend expectation but said 'we should prove it'; controlling is calculating in parallel; no jointly validated ROI/TCO model exists yet.

deal_movement.summary: Commercial negotiation deepened materially (CPO onsite 1 Jul, live pricing review 7 Jul) while the CRM tape moved down: scope carved to a 7-year S2C base with optional P2P, amount cut to 448.68K, forecast downgraded and close pushed to 23 Jul.

items.change: Pölki reviewed Zycus's revised pricing live; Merlin Intake moved to a transaction-based model (18,000 transactions 2027, unlimited users); buyer took sizing away for internal calculation

Vienna onsite (Teil 1 + 2) with CPO Flandorfer, Pölki, Koller and Eppich: 7-year S2C + optional P2P structure presented; SAP/Turkey resource constraint and digital-roadmap budget gate stated by the CPO
Amount reduced 650,000 to 448,680 as the procurement (P2P) module was carved out as optional
Tonkean surfaced as a bolt-on intake/orchestration alternative on the AI-integration alignment call
Forecast downgraded Best Case to Upside Key Deal; close date pushed 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-23
Testing feedback: Zycus ahead on Source-to-Contract and AI, rivals slightly ahead on P2P usability

day_summary.overall: Engelbert Pölki joined a live walkthrough of Zycus's revised pricing before the formal send. Zycus accepted Post's proposed volumes on the core S2C modules and introduced a transaction-based model for Merlin Intake (18,000 transactions in 2027 with unlimited users), which Pölki will size internally once Zycus defines what counts as a transaction. Pölki also relayed that his boss wants a new business case with best market prices before engaging the CIO, asked for an updated implementation offer for the new scope, and agreed to another catch-up within the week.

items.name: Live review of revised pricing with Pölki
items.summary: Core S2C volumes accepted at Post's proposed levels; Merlin Intake shifted to a capped transaction model that Post must validate; carryover of unused transactions raised as an open design question.

items.name: Business case and approval path
items.summary: Pölki's leadership will only move on a new business case ranked on the digital roadmap; Zycus offered an internal team to co-build it from Post's 2% savings-over-spend target; CEO/CIO executive connect offered and declined for now.

items.name: Implementation cost refresh requested
items.summary: Post asked for an updated implementation offer reflecting the new scope; Zycus to follow up with the EY partner this week.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the strongest live threat at near price parity (475K vs Zycus's 448.68K) and has run its own interface discussions with Post; Zycus holds a buyer-validated edge on Source-to-Contract and AI. Tonkean is a medium bolt-on threat that exploits Post's SAP resource shortage on the P2P side. The incumbents (Allocation Network for S2C, Wallmedien WPS for P2P) are being displaced, but the S2C incumbent contract can be extended a year, which makes deferral (do nothing until 2027) a real funded alternative while budget priorities are fought out on the digital roadmap.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Post was compromising on their process for Coupa to participate. Coupa offered 475K SaaS.
competitors.source: Next_Step__c note (21 Apr 2026); Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 1 (Post confirmed similar interface talks with Coupa)
competitors.how_we_win: Hold the price-transparency ground Pölki asked for (side-by-side matrix), lead with the buyer-validated S2C and agentic AI edge from the 11 Jun testing feedback, and convert the 7-year price-guaranteed structure into the safer choice on Pölki's price-security criterion.

competitors.name: Tonkean
competitors.quote: Competitor offering intake/orchestration as a bolt-on; perceived as attractive short-term solution to provide guided buying over current P2P.
competitors.source: Avoma 24 Jun 2026 AI-based Integration Alignment call (carried forward; canonical name per vendor dictionary, heard as 'Tronkeon')
competitors.how_we_win: Reinforce the platform argument already made on that call: a bolt-on overlay loses reporting depth, doubles maintenance across WPS plus overlay, and forfeits the integrated AI benefit the team asked to scale up.

competitors.name: Wallmedien
competitors.quote: Meine haben ganz schön gestaunt, als wir eine Spec von 2013 bekommen (Post on the WPS interface documentation).
competitors.how_we_win: Not a live bid: the P2P incumbent Post keeps short-term. Win by proving the WPS v4 API bridge works (Intake link-out as the floor) so retaining WPS temporarily does not block the Zycus suite decision.

competitors.name: Allocation Network
competitors.quote: Die derzeitige Lösung von Allocation Network wird als unzureichend empfunden, insbesondere wegen des schlechten Supports nach der Übernahme durch QAT.
competitors.how_we_win: Being displaced, but its contract can be extended one more year (Flandorfer, 1 Jul 2026), which funds the deferral option; make the cost of another incumbent year explicit in the business case.

competitors.name: Do-nothing (defer to 2027 on the digital roadmap)
competitors.quote: There is no real sense of urgency to change everything at the moment... the business cases are fighting against each other.
competitors.how_we_win: For this buyer, do-nothing means extending an S2C tool they call inadequate, keeping a 2013-spec-era P2P interface estate, and losing the pre-CE-migration window. Quantify that cost in the co-built business case so the roadmap ranking is won on numbers.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned on suite breadth, AI ambition and core S2C pricing; partially aligned on price security (the new Merlin Intake transaction cap must be proven safe) and on P2P usability; exposed on nothing structural except the implementation-cost picture, which is three months stale.

items.criterion: Price transparency and security over time
items.quote: Important to Pölki is price transparency and security over time, he needs to build a side-2-side comparison of the offers from Coupa and Zycus
items.source: Next_Step_History__c (21 May 2026); transaction-cap concern voiced Avoma 7 Jul 2026

items.criterion: One integrated suite for the whole group (Austria to Azerbaijan)
items.quote: We altogether in the end want to have one system, one suite, which we can use in a target picture in our entire group of companies.

items.criterion: Rollout flexibility given locked SAP/IT resources
items.quote: Not only a good pricing, but also the flexibility to support us in this quite special situation.
items.source: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 2 (Flandorfer); Zycus responded with 7-year S2C + optional P2P + 24-month price guarantee

items.quote: The team has asked for 50 to 70% upside (in AI event volumes).
items.source: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 2 (Amit, verbatim); buyer increased ANA event asks

items.quote: Some competitors had a slight advantage in P2P usability.
We only have the implementation costs offer from the first offer, 3 months ago, based on a new scope, on a new plan. Could you send us a new one?

items.requirement: Clear definition of a Merlin Intake 'transaction' and how it is measured, so Post can size the 18,000/year cap
items.quote: What would be helpful that we will have a good definition what is one transaction.
items.source: Avoma 7 Jul 2026 call (verbatim); Zycus promised it by 8 Jul evening

items.requirement: Updated implementation cost offer reflecting the new scope and plan (existing offer is 3 months stale)
items.quote: Could you send us a new one? That would be very helpful.
items.source: Avoma 7 Jul 2026 call (verbatim); Zycus to follow up with the EY partner this week

items.requirement: Business case support starting from Post's numbers, proving the expected 2% cost saving over spend
items.quote: We are expecting, for example, two percent cost saving over the spend, but we should prove it.

items.requirement: Total-cost scenario comparison: Zycus scenarios (S2C-now vs later, with interface and IT costs) against the current systems
items.quote: Dass er die Gesamtkosten kriegt und im Vergleich zu den aktuellen Systemen und die IT Kosten in den unterschiedlichen Szenarien.

items.requirement: Confirmation of what Zycus can do against Wallmedien WPS v4 APIs (catalog call-off, account-assignment changes) for the interim P2P bridge
items.quote: Sie müssen halt wissen, wo wir bei WPS stehen.
items.source: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 1 (verbatim); Eppich sent updated WPS documentation ~6 Jul

items.deliverable: Send the revised pricing spreadsheet in draft form right after the call
items.grounding_quote: I will send you the extra spreadsheet with the numbers right after this meeting.
items.source: Avoma 7 Jul 2026 call (verbatim); send not yet confirmed in the record

items.deliverable: Provide the written definition of a Merlin Intake transaction and how it is measured
items.grounding_quote: We need to provide you with what is a transaction which we can do tomorrow by tomorrow evening.

items.deliverable: Updated implementation proposal for the new scope, coordinated with the EY partner (Christian)
items.grounding_quote: We will follow up with him this week so that you will get a new proposal for implementation as well.

items.deliverable: Draft business case built from Post's numbers (2% savings over spend) for Pölki to adjust and take to controlling
items.grounding_quote: We've got an internal team that can build and help build business case... with your numbers, and then we can send it to you.

items.deliverable: Answer on carryover of unused Merlin Intake transactions into the next year
items.grounding_quote: If you don't use all your transactions in one year, can you carry that over to the next year as well?

items.deliverable: Wallmedien WPS v4 API verification and SAP partner-page link to Karin Eppich (expert back from vacation, follow-up Monday)
items.grounding_quote: Ich schick Ihnen, Frau Eppich, mal den Link auf die Partnerseite von SAP... ich meld mich spätestens Montag, sobald ich da Update hab.

items.deliverable: Internal calculation of annual Merlin Intake transaction volume and feedback on whether 18,000 suffices
items.grounding_quote: I have to calculate it internally... I will discuss the transaction figures with my team and come back to you.

items.deliverable: Date of the next digital-roadmap checkpoint where the business case will be ranked
items.grounding_quote: I will talk with the people with our digital road map when the next schedule is planned... and they can tell it to you.

items.deliverable: IT green light and joint session on the S2C interface topics so implementation costs can be made real
items.grounding_quote: Dass man endlich jetzt diese S2C Schnittstellenthematiken untersuchen können, gemeinsam mit der IT.
items.source: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 1 (verbatim); IT status meeting was set for 2 Jul

items.deliverable: Latest-decision date from Post for a Q4 2026 S2C start (Zycus asked for the 'latest, not earliest' commitment point)
items.grounding_quote: If we want to do this Q4 2026, when do you need the latest in order to be able to do that together with a partner?

items.area: Date of the digital-roadmap checkpoint that gates the decision
items.quote: I will talk with the people with our digital road map when the next schedule is planned.

items.area: CIO identity and stance: the IT gatekeeper is engaged only through Pölki and is not a Salesforce contact; direct access declined for now
items.quote: He don't want to get his CIO at the moment.

items.area: CFO position on the transaction/consumption cost structure raised on 1 Jul remains unresolved
items.quote: Concern about Anna event cost structure (invoicing for variable consumption).

items.area: Competitor fields vs reality: the live Coupa/Tonkean picture is fully evidenced in calls and Next Step notes even where the canonical competitor field is thin
Wallmedien WPS API depth (v4 capabilities) unconfirmed until the Zycus expert verifies
items.quote: Ohne das verifiziert zu haben, wird halt immer ein Disclaimer dabei sein.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is now an internal-selling problem more than a competitive one: the buyer team is bought in, the CPO is engaged but conditional, and the fight is for budget priority and IT capacity inside Austrian Post. The levers are the co-built business case, arming Pölki for the roadmap fight, and keeping Coupa neutralized on price security.
flags: Win the budget fight, not just the bake-off: co-build the business case with Post's controlling (2% savings over ~1B group spend, plus the cost of another incumbent year and the lost pre-CE-migration window) so the roadmap ranking is won on the buyer's own numbers.

Convert the declined exec connect into a triggered one: Flandorfer said 'maybe in the next step'; re-table the Woodcock/CEO-to-CIO session the moment the business case is delivered, with the AI SAP-mapping demo (70% integration-effort reduction claim emailed 22 Jun) as the agenda.

Close the price-security loophole before Coupa does: the Merlin Intake transaction cap is the one element that contradicts Pölki's stated criterion; land the definition, sizing and carryover answer this week so the side-by-side matrix stays in Zycus's favor.

Re-anchor the paper: the close date should back-plan from the roadmap checkpoint and the mid-August implementation start, not from 23 Jul; get a written 'window of choice' software-selection signal, which Post itself said it needs to give ('bräuchten wir zumindest ein Signal, dass wir softwareseitig der Window of Choice sind', 1 Jul).

items.name: Mathias Flandorfer
items.title: Chief Procurement Officer
items.sentiment: engaged but conditional: wants one group-wide suite yet demands a new business case and withholds CIO access until it exists
items.risk: If the business case does not rank high on the digital roadmap he defers the whole decision; his flexibility asks (7-year term, optional P2P) show intent to buy on his terms, not urgency.
items.source: OpportunityContactRole (Executive Sponsor); Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 2 (verbatim)

items.name: Engelbert Pölki
items.title: Manager Strategic Projects IT & Supply Chain
items.sentiment: positive and working: negotiates pricing, engaged controlling, escalates to his boss
items.risk: Being held back by his own leadership ('wir werden auch ein bissel hingehalten', 1 Jul); his advocacy has not yet converted into IT or CPO sign-off.
items.source: OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker, primary); Avoma 7 Jul 2026 and 1 Jul 2026 calls (verbatim)

items.name: Karin Eppich
items.title: Purchasing processes and systems
items.sentiment: positive: voiced preference for Zycus 16 Mar 2026, drives the technical/WPS track, sent updated WPS documentation ~6 Jul
items.risk: Functional-level influence only; cannot carry the budget fight or unblock IT capacity.
items.source: OpportunityContactRole; Next_Step_History__c (16 Mar 2026); Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 1

items.name: Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner
items.title: CFO
items.sentiment: neutral: financial gatekeeper with an open concern on the variable-consumption cost structure
items.risk: Her cost-structure concern from the 1 Jul onsite is unresolved; controlling (her org) is now co-calculating the business case, so she can make or kill the roadmap ranking.
items.source: SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c (economic buyer field); prior record, 1 Jul 2026 onsite

items.name: Melissa Koller
items.title: Head of Purchasing Procure2Pay
items.sentiment: positive: 'the solution will be located in my team and I am already looking forward to next steps' (1 Jul onsite)
items.risk: Owns the P2P estate that was just carved out as optional; if the P2P bridge to Wallmedien WPS is not credible her team's daily workload argues for the Tonkean-style bolt-on.

champion_strength.summary: Pölki is a vocal, active champion doing real internal work (pricing negotiation, controlling engagement, daily escalations to his boss), but he is explicitly being held back by his own leadership and has not yet converted advocacy into IT or CPO sign-off. The 7 Jul call shows the pattern continuing: he spoke to his boss half an hour before the call and came back with a condition, not an approval.
champion_strength.source: Avoma 7 Jul 2026 call (verbatim); Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 1 ('wir werden auch ein bissel hingehalten')

scope_change.from: Full S2P suite including procurement (P2P), amount 650,000
scope_change.to: 7-year Source-to-Contract base with the procurement (P2P) module optional and price-guaranteed for 24 months; amount 448,680
scope_change.detail: Carve-out executed 25 Jun 2026 (amount 650,000 to 448,680) to give Post rollout flexibility while SAP/IT resources are locked on the Turkey S/4HANA implementation; presented as the deal structure at the 1 Jul onsite.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the differentiator carrying this deal: three AI SKUs (Merlin, Merlin Intake, AppXtend), the buyer raised its own agentic-AI event volumes 50 to 70% above Zycus's offer, Zycus pitched AI-assisted SAP integration cutting internal effort ~70% (22 Jun email to Pölki), and the AI/S2C edge was buyer-validated in the 11 Jun testing feedback. The transaction-based pricing of that same AI is now the open commercial question.

ai_fit_signal.summary: The buyer increased its agentic-AI volume asks unprompted, engaged on the S2P/AI roadmap (9 Jun), and wants AI showcased to its CIO to unlock IT support; Flandorfer framed Post as 'an innovation company' betting on future AI value.

items.detail: The project must win an internal budget fight: business cases 'fighting against each other' on the digital roadmap, no urgency because Post is already highly digitalized, and the incumbent S2C contract extendable another year as a fallback.
items.source: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 2; Avoma 7 Jul 2026 call

items.detail: SAP team resources consumed by the Turkey S/4HANA rollout (and CE migrations next year); even a positive selection cannot mobilize without IT capacity, and the Wallmedien WPS v4 API bridge is still unverified.
items.source: Avoma 1 Jul 2026 onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2 (verbatim)

items.detail: CIO not engaged and the CEO/CIO executive connect was declined 'at the moment' (7 Jul); CFO's variable-consumption cost concern from 1 Jul unresolved.
The new transaction-capped Merlin Intake model collides with Pölki's price-security criterion until definition, sizing and carryover are settled; Coupa sits at near parity (475K).
Close date 23 Jul is 14 days out with no selection, no dated checkpoint, and a mid-August implementation start required for the end-of-year go-live Zycus promised.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is dense, senior and two-way (CPO onsite 1 Jul, live pricing review 7 Jul, buyer homework in motion), so the deal is not cooling. Confidence in the date, not the relationship, is what dropped: the rep downgraded the forecast and pushed the close on 22 Jun, and the buyer's own leadership has parked the decision behind an undated internal checkpoint.
items: Buyer accepted a same-week follow-up catch-up on the 7 Jul call and took two concrete homework items (transaction sizing, roadmap schedule).
Post itself acknowledged urgency on its side: 'jede Woche zählt mittlerweile' and 'eigentlich hätten wir jetzt schon zuschreiben wollen' (1 Jul onsite Teil 1).
Counter-signal: forecast downgraded and close pushed on 22 Jun; amount cut 25 Jun as P2P was carved out.

items.action: Deliver the written Merlin Intake transaction definition, measurement method and carryover answer to Pölki.
items.trigger: Promised by 8 Jul evening on the 7 Jul call; now overdue and blocking Post's internal sizing of the 18,000 cap.
items.expected_effect: Removes the one element contradicting Pölki's price-security criterion and keeps the side-by-side matrix vs Coupa in Zycus's favor.

items.action: Send the draft business case built on Post's 2% savings-over-spend target for controlling review.
items.trigger: Flandorfer will only advance the project on a new business case; Post's controlling is calculating in parallel now.
items.expected_effect: Arms Pölki and controlling to win the digital-roadmap ranking, which is the single gate on the whole decision.

items.action: Deliver the updated EY implementation offer for the new S2C-first scope, including AI-assisted integration effort reductions.
items.trigger: Pölki asked on 7 Jul; the only offer Post holds is 3 months stale and on the old scope; EY partner Christian back from vacation.
items.expected_effect: Completes the total-cost scenario comparison the CPO office needs and shrinks the IT-effort objection with the ~70% AI-mapping reduction already teased on 22 Jun.

items.action: Confirm Wallmedien WPS v4 API feasibility and send Karin Eppich the verified integration read.
items.trigger: Zycus's Wallmedien expert returned 3 Jul; Eppich sent updated WPS documentation ~6 Jul and is waiting.
items.expected_effect: Makes the interim P2P bridge credible, which removes Melissa Koller's operational objection and undercuts the Tonkean bolt-on narrative.

items.action: Get the digital-roadmap checkpoint date from Pölki and co-author a close plan back-planned from a mid-August implementation start.
items.trigger: Pölki committed on 7 Jul to find out when the next roadmap schedule is planned; the 23 Jul close date needs re-anchoring to reality.
items.expected_effect: Converts an undated internal gate into a mutual dated plan and gives the forecast a defensible close date.

items.action: Re-table the executive connect to Post's CIO, fronted by the AI SAP-analysis demo, once the business case lands.
items.trigger: Flandorfer declined the connect 'at the moment' but said it 'will be helpful maybe in the next step'; delivery of the business case is that next step.
items.expected_effect: Unlocks the IT gatekeeper whose resource sign-off gates both the roadmap ranking and the implementation start.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities 120d (2026-03-11 to 2026-07-07); field history 365d; Next_Step_History__c full trail deduped
gaps: Digital-roadmap checkpoint date unknown (buyer to supply)
CIO not identified as a Salesforce contact; stance known only via Pölki
CFO cost-structure concern from 1 Jul unresolved and not re-evidenced this sweep
Send-confirmation of the 7 Jul draft pricing spreadsheet not yet in the record

drivers: ✅ Confirmed in the final shortlist and the vendor Post is negotiating structure with: 7-year S2C contract, optional P2P and a 24-month price guarantee tabled at the CPO-attended Vienna onsite on 1 Jul 2026, followed by a live pricing review with Pölki on 7 Jul.

✅ Buyer-voiced preference and validation: Karin Eppich voiced preference for Zycus (16 Mar 2026) and the 11 Jun testing feedback put Zycus ahead on Source-to-Contract and AI, at a lower price than Coupa (448.68K vs 475K).
✅ Direct executive access achieved: CPO Mathias Flandorfer spent the 1 Jul onsite engaging on licence model, group-wide scaling and flexibility in person.

⚠️ No selection has been made and the field is still open: Flandorfer is comparing 'everybody who is now in the shortlist' with Coupa at near price parity. ► Land the transaction definition and business case this week so the side-by-side comparison Pölki is building resolves in Zycus's favor.

⚠️ The decision is gated on an undated internal budget checkpoint while SAP/IT resources are locked on the Turkey rollout, and the CIO connect was declined for now. ► Deliver the co-built business case, then re-table the executive-to-CIO session with the AI SAP-mapping demo as the door-opener.

⚠️ The quantified business case leadership demands does not exist: Pölki's 2% savings expectation is unproven and the forecast was downgraded to Upside on 22 Jun. ► Ship the draft business case to controlling by 16 Jul and co-author a close plan back-planned from the mid-August implementation start.

✅ Two deep buyer sessions in the last 8 days: the CPO-attended Vienna onsite (1 Jul, Teil 1 + 2) and a live pricing review with Pölki (7 Jul) where the buyer took sizing homework away and accepted a same-week follow-up.

✅ Buyer is working between meetings: Pölki spoke to his boss about the deal 30 minutes before the 7 Jul call, engaged controlling on the business case, and Eppich sent updated WPS documentation around 6 Jul.
✅ Dense two-way June cadence: pricing and integration sessions on 9, 11, 16, 24 and 25 Jun plus buyer replies on the spend-file audit (8-9 Jun); the Next Step trail is fresh and forward-dated.

⚠️ The CRM tape moved against the deal in the window: forecast downgraded Best Case to Upside Key Deal and close pushed 30 Jun to 23 Jul (22 Jun), amount cut 650K to 448.68K (25 Jun). ► Re-anchor the close date to the roadmap checkpoint so the next move on the tape is credible, not another slip.

⚠️ Forward motion now depends on an undated buyer-side gate: the digital-roadmap checkpoint has no date and leadership is explicitly holding the decision. ► Get the checkpoint date from Pölki within 14 days and back-plan the mutual close plan from the mid-August implementation start.

⚠️ Zycus owes the fuel for the next cycle: the transaction definition (promised 8 Jul) is overdue and the updated implementation offer is pending with EY. ► Clear both this week; the buyer cannot do its homework without them.

Secure a written 'window of choice' software-selection signal and co-author a mutual close plan back-planned from the mid-August implementation start

sum.headline: No change in the last 24h.
supporting: In the last 48h (7 Jul): Pölki reviewed Zycus's revised pricing live, accepted that core S2C volumes now match Post's proposal, and took the new 18,000-transaction Merlin Intake model away for internal sizing, though his leadership will only advance the deal once a new business case clears Austrian Post's internal roadmap checkpoint.
Also in that window: Post asked for an updated implementation offer on the new scope, and a CEO/CIO executive connect was offered and declined for now.
